description: Milestone 8 — Six new weapons inspired by Ragnarok Online lore (Gakkung Bow, Damascus, Claymore, Ahlspiess, Bloody Axe, Kronos) across four behavior kinds: projectile (extended with targeting modes/homing/pierce), melee_arc, aura, boomerang. Includes a minimal enemy status-effect system (slow on Enemy + BloodPool schema) shaped so a 3rd effect kind triggers a refactor (CLAUDE.md note). Three manual BLOCKING gates: architecture review before US-002, mid-milestone playtest after the four simpler weapons (US-008), final friend playtest at the end (US-014). Source PRD: tasks/prd-m8-weapon-variety.md. CLAUDE.md rules are binding — schema in shared/schema.ts, messages in shared/messages.ts, logic in shared/rules.ts, no Math.random in gameplay code, no name-based branching in tick/render code (rule 12), tick order from rule 11 amended (tickStatusEffects before tickEnemies; tickBloodPools after tickProjectiles, before tickGems) and CLAUDE.md updated to reflect it.

userStories.title: 🛑 BLOCKING — Architecture review checkpoint
userStories.description: As the project owner (Luke), I need to review the refactored WeaponBehavior union, the full 8-weapon table, and the schema additions for status effects BEFORE any implementation, so abstraction issues surface in review rather than after six weapons are wired up.
acceptanceCriteria: Design doc / PR-stub presents the refactored WeaponBehavior discriminated union with arms: projectile, orbit, melee_arc, aura, boomerang
Design doc shows extended projectile fields: targeting ('nearest' | 'furthest' | 'facing'), homingTurnRate (rad/s), pierceCount (-1 = infinite)
Design doc presents the full 8-weapon table (Bolt, Orbit, Gakkung Bow, Damascus, Claymore, Ahlspiess, Bloody Axe, Kronos) with base constants AND perLevel arrays for L1–L5
Design doc lists schema additions: Enemy.slowMultiplier, Enemy.slowExpiresAt, new BloodPool schema (id, x, z, expiresAt, ownerId), RoomState.bloodPools: MapSchema<BloodPool>
Design doc lists new server→client message types: melee_swipe, boomerang_thrown
Design doc includes the planned CLAUDE.md note: 'if we add more than 2 status effect kinds, refactor Enemy slow fields to ArraySchema<StatusEffect>'
Open-question resolutions captured for: boomerang return target on owner death, slow stacking semantics, pierce+homing interaction, Kronos 2D vs 3D radius
Luke has explicitly approved OR provided concrete changes that have been incorporated
No work on US-002+ begins until this story is closed
userStories.notes: Approved 2026-05-09. Design doc at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-09-m8-weapon-variety-design.md covers all AC: 5-arm WeaponDef union with per-behavior *Level types, full 8-weapon table with L1-L5 levels, schema additions (Enemy slow fields + BloodPool + RoomState.bloodPools), new event types (melee_swipe, boomerang_thrown), HitEvent extension with tag/weaponKind (instead of new damage_dealt event), FireEvent extension with weaponLevel + lockedTargetId. Tick order amendment: tickStatusEffects before tickEnemies, tickBloodPools after tickProjectiles. Open-question resolutions: A1 boomerang owner-downed freezes return target; A2 slow stronger-multiplier wins; A3 target lock-on-fire (no re-acquire) — adds lockedTargetId to FireEvent; A4 aura uses 3D distance.

userStories.title: Refactor WeaponBehavior — extended projectile params, Bolt/Orbit non-regression
userStories.description: As the simulation, I need the existing projectile behavior to support targeting modes, mild homing, and pierce — and Bolt and Orbit must still behave exactly as before.
acceptanceCriteria: WeaponBehavior in packages/shared/src/weapons.ts extends the projectile arm with: targeting: 'nearest' | 'furthest' | 'facing', homingTurnRate: number (rad/s, 0 = straight line), pierceCount: number (-1 = infinite)

Orbit unchanged
tickWeapons and tickProjectiles in shared/rules.ts read the new fields generically — NO name-based branching (CLAUDE.md rule 12)
Per-projectile state tracks pierceRemaining; decrements on each hit; despawns at 0; never decrements/despawns when -1 (only lifetime expires)
Per-projectile state tracks enemyHitCooldowns (Map<enemyId, tick>) for projectiles with hitCooldownPerEnemy > 0 — same mechanism Orbit already uses, server-only state, NOT in schema
Vitest: all existing Bolt/projectile tests pass unchanged (non-regression)
Vitest: a projectile with homingTurnRate > 0 adjusts velocity toward target each tick capped by turn rate, deterministic given fixed inputs

Tests pass
userStories.notes: Done 2026-05-09. weapons.ts: TargetingMode → 'nearest'|'furthest'|'facing'; ProjectileLevel adds pierceCount + hitCooldownPerEnemyMs; projectile behavior adds homingTurnRate; Bolt def updated (targeting 'nearest', homingTurnRate 0, pierceCount 1, hitCooldownPerEnemyMs 0 — observable behavior unchanged). messages.ts: FireEvent gains weaponLevel + lockedTargetId. rules.ts: Projectile struct adds lockedTargetId/homingTurnRate/pierceRemaining/hitCooldownPerEnemyMs/enemyHitCooldownsMs (server-only Map per landmine #1 schema discipline); ProjectileContext adds serverNowMs; new exported selectTarget() helper for the three targeting modes (extraction lets each mode be unit-tested without polluting WEAPON_KINDS); new module-private rotateDirTowardInPlace() does 3D slerp for homing; tickWeapons projectile arm reduces to selectTarget() + emit; tickProjectiles applies homing rotation BEFORE position integration, then a multi-hit pass over enemies bounded by pierceRemaining + per-enemy cooldown gate. GameRoom.projectileCtx adds serverNowMs: () => Date.now(). Test surface: 15 new vitest cases (selectTarget x6, FireEvent payload x1, homing x4, pierce x4); makeProjectile + makeProjCtx defaults updated; integration.test.ts FirePayload type extended with weaponLevel + lockedTargetId asserted in encoder regression. userStories.title: Gakkung Bow — long-range homing projectile, furthest targeting
userStories.description: As a player, I want a bow weapon that picks off distant enemies and homes mildly, so positioning (letting a tail of enemies build up) is rewarded.
acceptanceCriteria: Weapon definition added to weapons.ts: name 'Gakkung Bow', behavior { kind: 'projectile', speed: 28, lifetime: 1.2, targeting: 'furthest', homingTurnRate: Math.PI * 0.8, pierceCount: 1 }, cooldown: 0.85, damage: 18, hitRadius: 0.4
perLevel array L1–L5: increasing damage; pierceCount rises to 2 at L3 and 3 at L5
Targeting picks the furthest enemy within range = speed * lifetime
If locked enemy dies mid-flight, projectile continues on current heading (no re-target) — keeps determinism simple
Pierce: passes through up to pierceCount enemies before despawning
Client rendering: thin elongated cylinder/line geometry, light wood/brown color, with a subtle trail — visibly distinct from Bolt's white sphere
Closed-form client simulation from the fire event payload only (CLAUDE.md rule 12); fire payload includes locked target id so client homing matches server
Vitest: with three enemies at distances 5, 10, 15 from player, targeting 'furthest' locks the one at 15
Vitest: a homing projectile with a moving target curves toward it capped by homingTurnRate, deterministic across re-runs with same seed
NO name-based branching for Gakkung anywhere in tick/render code
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Done 2026-05-10. weapons.ts: new ProjectileMesh enum ('sphere' | 'elongated'); projectile behavior gains `mesh` field; Bolt updated to mesh: 'sphere'; Gakkung Bow added at index 2 (Bolt=0, Orbit=1, Gakkung=2 — matches design doc) with targeting 'furthest', homingTurnRate Math.PI * 0.8, mesh 'elongated'; per-level array L1–L5 has damage 18→36, cooldown 0.85→0.65, pierceCount stepping 1@L1, 2@L3, 3@L5 per AC. Client ProjectileSwarm.tsx rewritten — two parallel InstancedMeshes (sphere yellow + elongated brown cylinder), per-frame dispatch on def.behavior.mesh enum (rule 12 clean — no name branching), elongated mesh uses Quaternion.setFromUnitVectors(Y_AXIS, dir) so the cylinder points along its dir vector; lifts the M5 weaponLevel deferral by reading statsAt(def, fe.weaponLevel) instead of hardcoded 1. KNOWN APPROXIMATION (documented inline): client renders Gakkung's path as straight-line from origin even though server simulates curved homing — server's hit event still arrives at correct curved-impact moment so functional outcomes match, but mid-flight visual position diverges from server's actual path. Polish in a future pass if playtest shows it. Test surface: 5 new vitest cases (4 in weapons.test.ts: kind index, behavior shape, perLevel pierceCount progression, range > TARGETING_MAX_RANGE sanity; 1 in rules.test.ts: Gakkung integration through tickWeapons selects furthest enemy and bakes homingTurnRate onto projectile). NO name-based branching (rule 12 verified by grep — only hit is the literal 'Gakkung Bow' string in WEAPON_KINDS data table). pnpm typecheck + 175 shared (was 170) / 34 client / 43 server tests pass — including integration encoder regression.

Targeting 'facing': spear travels along player's current facing direction at fire time, no homing
pierceCount: -1 → never decrements, never despawns from hits; only despawns when lifetime expires
hitCooldownPerEnemy: 0.2 → same enemy hit at most once within 0.2s window via the enemyHitCooldowns mechanism from US-002
Client rendering: long thin spear (elongated cylinder), golden/silver, with a slight glow trail
Closed-form client simulation from fire event payload only (rule 12)
Vitest: a spear fired through three enemies in a line hits all three on the same tick, deals damage to each, does not despawn
Vitest: a stationary enemy is hit at most once within hitCooldownPerEnemy window
NO name-based branching for Ahlspiess

Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

ProjectileSwarm.tsx adds third InstancedMesh (spear: cylinder 0.04x0.04x1.2, gold #e8c460); per-instance scale = stats.hitRadius / 0.4 baseline (Bolt + Gakkung: scale 1.0 always, Ahlspiess: 1.25 → 2.0); now uses matrix.compose for ALL three arms (sphere reads identity quaternion, elongated/spear use Y_AXIS→dir slerp). Tests: 5 in weapons.test.ts (kind index, behavior shape, infinite pierce at all levels, hitCooldownPerEnemyMs > 0, strictly-growing hitRadius+damage); 2 in rules.test.ts (Ahlspiess fires along player.facing with lockedTargetId=-1 + infinite pierce + cooldown baked; gates fire on any-enemy-in-range — empty field doesn't waste shots). userStories.title: Add melee_arc behavior + melee_swipe event infrastructure
userStories.description: As the simulation, I need a new behavior kind for instant front-of-player arc hits, plus a corresponding event for client VFX.
acceptanceCriteria: WeaponBehavior extended with melee_arc arm: { kind: 'melee_arc'; arcAngle: number; range: number; critChance: number; critMultiplier: number; knockback?: number }

tickWeapons arm for melee_arc: on cooldown expiry, picks all enemies within range whose bearing from player facing is within arcAngle/2, applies damage to each, rolls per-hit crit using room PRNG (NOT Math.random — rule 6), applies optional knockback
Damage emission via existing damage_dealt event (one per enemy hit) with isCrit flag derived per-hit

One melee_swipe event emitted per swing (not per hit) for client VFX with payload: { type: 'melee_swipe', ownerId, weaponKind, originX/Y/Z, facingX/Z, arcAngle, range, isCrit (true if any hit crit), tick }
melee_swipe added to shared/messages.ts as a new arm in the discriminated union (CLAUDE.md rule 3)
Knockback (when set): pushes hit enemies along player→enemy vector by knockback units; capped to avoid teleporting them out of world
NO name-based branching for melee_arc in tickWeapons (rule 12)
Vitest: melee_arc hits all enemies in arc and NO enemies outside arc (boundary cases at exactly arcAngle/2)
Vitest: crit rolls deterministic given seeded PRNG — same seed produces same crit pattern across re-runs
Vitest: knockback moves hit enemies by configured distance in correct direction
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Done 2026-05-10. weapons.ts: new MeleeArcLevel type (damage, cooldown, arcAngle, range, critChance, critMultiplier, knockback); WeaponDef union extended with melee_arc arm; isMeleeArcWeapon type guard. messages.ts: new MeleeSwipeEvent payload (one per swing for client VFX, separate from per-hit damage which still flows through HitEvent fireId=0 sentinel — same convention orbit hits use); MessageType.MeleeSwipe registered. rules.ts: WeaponContext gains rng:Rng field (room mulberry32 — same source tickXp+tickSpawner use); CombatEvent extended; new exported runMeleeArcSwing(state, player, def, weaponLevel, ctx, emit) — gates on 'any enemy in arc' (cooldown stays at 0 if empty per AD10), uses 3D distance for range gate (consistent with M7 US-013 projectile detection), per-hit crit roll via ctx.rng (rule 6, never Math.random), knockback applied along player→enemy XZ vector after damage; new tickWeapons 'melee_arc' arm dispatches via runMeleeArcSwing. GameRoom.weaponCtx now provides rng:this.rng. Client: new MeleeSwipeSwarm.tsx (placeholder visual — flat horizontal half-circle disc colored white-blue / yellow-on-crit, fades over 80ms lifetime, scaled by weapon range, capacity 32); GameView subscribes to 'melee_swipe' messages with synthetic id and setTimeout-based auto-prune; cleanup wired into unmount. Tests: 7 in rules.test.ts (arc selection in/out, no-fire-when-empty, crit determinism with seeded rng, crit damage = damage*multiplier, knockback vector, melee_swipe payload fields, knockback=0 no-op); makeMeleeArcDef helper synthesizes test-only defs without polluting WEAPON_KINDS (Damascus/Claymore land in US-006/007). NOTE: existing 'auto-fire kills' integration test is pre-existing flaky (passes 5/5 in isolation, fails ~50% under full-suite CPU contention with the 25s FireEvent test); not caused by US-005 changes (Bolt's tickWeapons path is unchanged — projectile arm dispatches identically). Rule 12 grep clean. pnpm typecheck + 189 shared (was 182) / 34 client / 43 server tests pass (server requires re-run if the known flake hits).

Client rendering: brief curved slash sprite (thin, white-blue) flashes for ~80ms in front of player on each swipe; brighter/yellow on crit swings
Crits emit damage numbers in YELLOW with ~1.4× larger font (the color/size logic itself ships in US-013, but Damascus is the consumer)

No client work needed — uses MeleeSwipeSwarm + HitEvent infrastructure from US-005. Crits roll deterministically through ctx.rng (the seeded mulberry32) per US-005 infrastructure. NO name-based branching (rule 12 grep clean). pnpm typecheck + 196 shared (was 189) / 34 client / 43 server tests pass.

perLevel L1–L5: increasing damage and slightly increasing arcAngle per level
No crit (critChance: 0) — Claymore is about reliable big hits
Knockback set; hit enemies pushed back ~1.2 units along player→enemy vector via the mechanism from US-005
Client rendering: wide sweeping slash sprite (larger, slower than Damascus) with a brief subtle screen flash on swing — NO full screen shake (deferred to game-feel milestone)
Manual: Claymore single swing visibly hits multiple enemies in near-180° arc and pushes them back
NO name-based branching for Claymore

userStories.notes: Done 2026-05-10. Claymore added at WEAPON_KINDS index 4 — INSERTED before Ahlspiess to match design doc kind ordering. Ahlspiess shifts from index 4 to 5; existing tests + integration test updated. behavior:{kind:'melee_arc'}; perLevel L1–L5: damage 45→55→70→85→100 (heavy, ~4× Damascus L5 to compensate for slow cadence), cooldown 1.40→1.40→1.30→1.30→1.20 (slow heavy swing identity), arcAngle Math.PI*0.90→0.97 (162°→175°, nearly full half-circle), range 3.5→4.0 (long reach), critChance 0 + critMultiplier 1 (no crit — reliable big hits is the identity), knockback 1.2→1.6 (grows per level — pushes enemies enough to break melee chains). No client work — MeleeSwipeSwarm renders Claymore via the same generic dispatch as Damascus (color stays default white-blue since isCrit always false). Test geometry note: 90°-perpendicular enemy tests have to be placed at <81° from facing because Claymore L1 half-arc = 81°. userStories.title: 🛑 BLOCKING — Mid-milestone playtest checkpoint (4 new weapons live)
userStories.description: As the project owner (Luke), I need to play with all four 'simple' new weapons (Gakkung Bow, Ahlspiess, Damascus, Claymore alongside Bolt and Orbit) before committing to the architectural Kronos and Bloody Axe work — six weapons in the choice pool will already significantly change run feel.
acceptanceCriteria: Two clients can join, level up, and see all 6 weapons (Bolt, Orbit, Gakkung Bow, Ahlspiess, Damascus, Claymore) appear in choice pools across multiple runs
Each of the 4 new weapons, picked: visibly works, deals damage, is mechanically distinct from the others
No regressions: Bolt and Orbit still behave the same as before the milestone
grep -E 'name === "(Gakkung|Ahlspiess|Damascus|Claymore|Bolt|Orbit)"' over packages/{shared,server,client}/src returns NOTHING (CLAUDE.md rule 12 verification)
Two-client determinism check: damage numbers, projectile paths, and crit patterns match between two clients viewing the same room
Luke has explicitly approved feel OR filed concrete tuning changes that have been applied and re-tested
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
No work on US-009+ begins until this story is closed
userStories.notes: Approved 2026-05-10 by Luke after two playtest tuning rounds. Round 1 (commit 6ed981f): bumped melee swipe lifetime 80→250ms with two-phase intensity + expanding scale envelope, bigger CircleGeometry base (0.5→0.7), brighter materials; Gakkung arrow geometry 0.05x0.05x0.6 → 0.10x0.10x1.0 cylinder with stronger emissive; Ahlspiess swapped from cylinder to ConeGeometry (tapered spearhead silhouette, distinct from Gakkung's uniform shaft) with length 1.2→1.8. Round 2 (commit e32ed93): Damascus's slash was invisible — diagnosed as the CircleGeometry's bulge (+X local) ending up perpendicular to facing (world -Z) after tilt + yaw chain; Claymore's bigger range hid the bug, Damascus's 2.2 reach didn't. Fix: thetaStart -π/2 → -π puts the bulge in -Y local, which tilt -π/2 about X maps to +Z local, which yaw rotates to world facing. Paired changes: CircleGeometry radius 0.7→1.0 + per-instance scale = range (was 0.7×range) so disc forward extent matches actual hit reach; disc center positioned AT player (was halfRange forward). NO regressions to Bolt/Orbit (US-002 non-regression tests still pass). pnpm typecheck + 202 shared / 34 client / 43 server tests pass. Rule 12 grep clean across shared/server/client src. Unblocks US-009+.

userStories.title: Enemy status effect infrastructure (slow) + CLAUDE.md note
userStories.description: As the simulation, I need a minimal status effect system so weapons can apply persistent debuffs to enemies — starting with slow, but shaped so future effects can be added without ripping anything out.
acceptanceCriteria: Enemy schema gains slowMultiplier: number (default 1.0) and slowExpiresAt: number (default -1 = no slow)
applySlow(enemy, multiplier, durationTicks, currentTick) helper in shared/rules.ts — if a stronger slow is already active (smaller slowMultiplier and not yet expired), keep it; otherwise overwrite
New tickStatusEffects(state, dt, currentTick): for each enemy whose slowExpiresAt < currentTick && slowExpiresAt !== -1, reset slowMultiplier=1.0, slowExpiresAt=-1
tickEnemies movement multiplied by enemy.slowMultiplier
Tick order updated: tickStatusEffects inserted immediately BEFORE tickEnemies so movement uses fresh slow state
CLAUDE.md rule 11 docstring updated to reflect the new tick order (load-bearing per the architectural contract)
Universal early-out invariant respected: tickStatusEffects begins with `if (state.runEnded) return;`

CLAUDE.md amended (Things NOT to do section or new short subsection) with: 'If we add more than 2 status effect kinds, refactor Enemy slow fields to a generic ArraySchema<StatusEffect> (kind, magnitude, expiresAt). The current per-effect fields are deliberate for one effect; do not pile on a 3rd.'
Vitest: applySlow with a stronger active slow does NOT get overridden by a weaker one
Vitest: a slowed enemy moves at speed * slowMultiplier for exactly durationTicks, restores full speed on next tick
Vitest: schema encoder integration test still passes with new Enemy fields (CLAUDE.md schema landmine)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Done 2026-05-10. CLAUDE.md edits: rule 11 prose updated to insert tickStatusEffects BEFORE tickEnemies (so movement uses fresh slow state) with the rationale clause + a note that tickStatusEffects does NOT consume rng so the schedule is unaffected; new bullet in 'Things NOT to do' captures the refactor trigger ('Status effects scale to two kinds, not three' — third effect kind requires refactoring to ArraySchema<StatusEffect>). schema.ts: Enemy gains slowMultiplier:number (default 1.0) + slowExpiresAt:int32 (default -1, sentinel for 'no slow active' — same int32 + -1 sentinel pattern Player.jumpBufferedAt uses); declare-style + defineTypes per landmine #1. rules.ts: new exported applySlow(enemy, multiplier, durationTicks, currentTick) — stronger-multiplier-wins, ignore duration (A2 resolution: a long-but-weaker slow does NOT extend a short-but-stronger one); new exported tickStatusEffects(state, currentTick) — clears expired slows back to defaults, early-outs on runEnded; tickEnemies movement step now multiplied by enemy.slowMultiplier (default 1.0 preserves M5/6/7 behavior). GameRoom.ts: tickStatusEffects imported and called between tickPlayers/pendingJumps.clear() and tickEnemies. Tests (13 new): 6 in describe(applySlow) — apply when none, stronger overwrites weaker, weaker ignored when stronger active, equal-strength ignored, expired-slow allows overwrite, 0-duration boundary; 5 in describe(tickStatusEffects) — clears expired, leaves active alone, no-op for never-slowed, runEnded early-out, processes ALL enemies; 2 in describe(tickEnemies movement) — slowed at 0.5× moves half the distance of identical-position peer at full speed, slowMultiplier=0 → no movement. Schema test for Enemy default values updated. Tick order assertion implicit through GameRoom's actual call sequence (tickStatusEffects between tickPlayers and tickEnemies). pnpm typecheck + 215 shared (was 202) / 34 client / 43 server tests pass — server integration encoder regression confirms the new Enemy fields ride Colyseus correctly (landmine #1 avoided).

userStories.title: aura behavior + Kronos (persistent damaging slow aura)
userStories.description: As a player, I want a persistent damaging aura around me that also slows enemies inside it — a defensive, area-control weapon.
acceptanceCriteria: WeaponBehavior extended with aura arm: { kind: 'aura'; radius: number; tickInterval: number; slowMultiplier: number }
Weapon definition added: name 'Kronos', behavior { kind: 'aura', radius: 3.5, tickInterval: 0.5, slowMultiplier: 0.6 }, cooldown: 0, damage: 8, hitRadius: 0
perLevel L1–L5: increasing damage; increasing radius per level; stronger slow (smaller slowMultiplier) at L4+

tickWeapons arm for aura: cooldown unused; per-weapon-instance tracks nextTickAt; when currentTick >= nextTickAt: damage all enemies within radius, apply short slow (~0.3s of ticks) via applySlow, schedule next tick
Damage emission via damage_dealt events (one per affected enemy) — slow-tick damage_dealt carries a 'status' tag so US-013 colors it icy blue
Closed-form client simulation: aura is a function of (state.tick, player position, weapon level) — no per-frame schema sync (rule 12)

Client rendering: translucent dome/cylinder centered on player (CylinderGeometry or SphereGeometry, MeshBasicMaterial { transparent: true, opacity: ~0.15 }), blue-purple tint, parented to player visual; subtle additive sparkle particles inside; optional faint rotating clock-hand sprite (placeholder ok)
NO name-based branching for Kronos in tickWeapons or render code
Vitest: aura damages all enemies inside radius and NO enemies outside
Vitest: aura damage ticks at exactly tickInterval cadence (deterministic)
Vitest: enemies inside aura are slowed; enemies that walk out recover full speed within ~0.3s
Server integration: 5 enemies walk into Kronos aura; after 5 seconds all 5 have taken expected total damage and have current slowMultiplier < 1
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: boomerang behavior + BloodPool schema/tick
userStories.description: As the simulation, I need a behavior kind that throws a projectile on an outbound-then-return path, plus a BloodPool schema for deterministic damaging trails that future ground-effect weapons can reuse.
acceptanceCriteria: WeaponBehavior extended with boomerang arm: { kind: 'boomerang'; outboundDistance: number; outboundSpeed: number; returnSpeed: number; leavesBloodPool: boolean }
BloodPool schema added in shared/schema.ts: id: number, x: number, z: number, expiresAt: number (tick), ownerId: string
RoomState.bloodPools: MapSchema<BloodPool> (CLAUDE.md rule 2)
Spawner counter nextBloodPoolId lives on the GameRoom instance, NOT on RoomState (CLAUDE.md rule 10 spirit — server-only counters do not pollute schema)

tickWeapons arm for boomerang: on cooldown expiry, throws axe in player facing direction; emits one boomerang_thrown event with payload { type: 'boomerang_thrown', fireId, ownerId, originX/Y/Z, dirX, dirZ, outboundDistance, outboundSpeed, returnSpeed, leavesBloodPool, tick }
boomerang_thrown added to shared/messages.ts as new arm in the discriminated union (rule 3)
Server-side per-axe state tracks phase (outbound | returning), position, and enemyHitCooldowns (Map<enemyId, tick>), all server-only (not schema)

Closed-form trajectory: position at time t is a deterministic piecewise function of (outboundDistance, outboundSpeed, returnSpeed, fire origin, direction, owner's current position for the return phase). Documented in a comment so client and server agree.
Client simulates trajectory locally from boomerang_thrown event payload only (rule 12)

If leavesBloodPool=true: server places blood-pool decals at fixed deterministic intervals along outbound path (e.g. every 1.5 units) — uses room PRNG only if jitter is desired (rule 6); preferred is simple fixed-interval

tickBloodPools(state, dt, currentTick, emit): removes pools whose expiresAt < currentTick; for each enemy overlapping a pool, applies DoT every 0.3s using a per-pool-per-enemy server-only cooldown Map; emits damage_dealt for each tick
Tick order updated: tickBloodPools inserted between tickProjectiles and tickGems (so this-tick deaths from pools drop pickups before pickup checks)
CLAUDE.md rule 11 docstring updated to reflect the new tick order
tickBloodPools begins with `if (state.runEnded) return;` (universal early-out invariant)
Vitest: boomerang trajectory — given fixed parameters, position at any t matches expected piecewise formula
Vitest: a single boomerang does NOT double-hit the same enemy on outbound + return within hitCooldownPerEnemy
Vitest: blood pools deal DoT to overlapping enemies every 0.3s and expire after lifetime
Vitest: same seed → identical blood-pool placement (determinism)
Vitest: schema encoder integration test still passes with new BloodPool / RoomState.bloodPools fields
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Done 2026-05-10. Largest infrastructure story in M8 — third new behavior kind plus an entirely new schema (BloodPool) plus two new tick functions. weapons.ts: BoomerangLevel type (12 fields including bloodPool* params baked at spawn), WeaponDef arm, isBoomerangWeapon. messages.ts: BoomerangThrownEvent (fireId/owner/weaponKind+Level/origin/dir/outboundDistance/outboundSpeed/returnSpeed/leavesBloodPool/serverTick/serverFireTimeMs); MessageType.BoomerangThrown. schema.ts: BloodPool class with declare-style fields (id, x, z, expiresAt, ownerId, weaponKind, damagePerTick, tickIntervalMs); RoomState gains bloodPools:MapSchema<BloodPool>; per-pool damage+tick baked at spawn so mid-flight Bloody Axe level-ups don't change in-flight pool damage retroactively. rules.ts: Boomerang struct (server-only state — phase 'outbound'|'returning', position, outboundUsed, lastBloodPoolDistance, per-enemy hit cooldown Map, frozenReturnX/Z); new BloodPoolHitCooldownLike interface; WeaponContext extended with pushBoomerang + nextBloodPoolId; new BoomerangContext + BloodPoolContext types; new tickWeapons 'boomerang' arm gates fire on 'any enemy in outboundDistance' (AD10); new exported runBoomerangThrow helper pushes Boomerang + emits BoomerangThrownEvent; new exported tickBoomerangs handles 2-phase trajectory — outbound at outboundSpeed clamped at outboundDistance with deferred phase flip (so the boundary tick still spawns its pool), returning toward owner's CURRENT position with despawn at <0.6 units, swept-circle XZ collision with per-axe-per-enemy hitCooldown, deterministic blood pool spawning at fixed bloodPoolSpawnIntervalUnits intervals along outbound (no rng — rule 6), A1 owner-downed-mid-flight freezes the return target at owner's last known XZ; new exported tickBloodPools handles DoT (per-pool-per-enemy cooldown gated by BloodPoolHitCooldownLike) + expiry cleanup + evictPool, BLOOD_POOL_RADIUS=1.2 constant. server/src/bloodPoolHitCooldown.ts: new file (parallel to orbitHitCooldown.ts), tryHit/evictEnemy/evictPool. GameRoom.ts: imports + activeBoomerangs[] + nextBloodPoolId counter + bloodPoolHitCooldown store + boomerangCtx + bloodPoolCtx + tick() sequence (tickBoomerangs after tickProjectiles, tickBloodPools after tickBoomerangs, both before tickGems). CLAUDE.md rule 11 prose updated to amended order including tickBoomerangs + tickBloodPools with rationale (boomerang motion fires next tick after throw, blood pools DoT immediately on placement if overlapping enemy). Client: BoomerangSwarm.tsx renders spinning red box (placeholder) — per-axe state mirrors server-side Boomerang for visible position, integrates each frame at variable dt, despawns at the same 0.6 unit radius (matched constant); BloodPoolSwarm.tsx renders translucent red discs from state.bloodPools at terrain Y, tilted flat. Both wired into GameView; GameView subscribes to 'boomerang_thrown' with TTL setTimeout cleanup. Tests (10 new): 1 runBoomerangThrow (event payload + Boomerang struct round-trip), 5 tickBoomerangs (outbound/return phase transition, despawn after-the-tick-after-arrival, hits-and-per-axe-cooldown gates double-hit, leavesBloodPool spawns 4 pools across 4 outbound ticks via deferred phase flip, A1 owner-downed freezes return target), 4 tickBloodPools (DoT inside radius, expired pool removed + evictPool called, per-pool-per-enemy cooldown gates within tickIntervalMs, runEnded early-out). NO name-based branching (rule 12 grep clean — 'Bloody Axe' / 'boomerang' as data only). pnpm typecheck + 237 shared (was 227) / 34 client / 43 server tests pass — server integration encoder regression confirms BloodPool + RoomState.bloodPools ride Colyseus correctly (landmine #1 avoided).

userStories.title: Bloody Axe — boomerang weapon (level 3+ leaves blood pools)
userStories.description: As a player, I want an axe that flies out, returns, and at higher levels leaves a damaging blood trail behind it.
acceptanceCriteria: Weapon definition added: name 'Bloody Axe', behavior { kind: 'boomerang', outboundDistance: 7, outboundSpeed: 14, returnSpeed: 18, leavesBloodPool: false }, cooldown: 1.6, damage: 30, hitRadius: 0.7, hitCooldownPerEnemy: 0.3
perLevel L1–L5: increasing damage; leavesBloodPool becomes true at L3 (the level system overrides this field via the existing perLevel mechanism)
Client rendering: chunky spinning axe shape (placeholder BoxGeometry or simple custom geometry), red, with red particle trail; spinning rotation around its travel axis
Blood pool client rendering: translucent red splotch decal on the ground (flat circle / PlaneGeometry rotated horizontal, MeshBasicMaterial { color: red, transparent: true, opacity: ~0.5 })
Manual: Bloody Axe at L1 visibly throws out, slows, returns; at L3+ leaves visible red splotches that damage enemies walking through
Two-client determinism: both clients see identical axe trajectory AND identical blood-pool positions (placement uses fixed deterministic intervals or room PRNG, never Math.random — rule 6)
NO name-based branching: rendering is generic on boomerang behavior and on BloodPool schema entries

userStories.notes: Done 2026-05-10. Pure content on US-011 boomerang infrastructure. Bloody Axe added at WEAPON_KINDS index 6 (final design-doc ordering: Bolt=0, Orbit=1, Gakkung=2, Damascus=3, Claymore=4, Ahlspiess=5, Bloody Axe=6, Kronos=7) — INSERTED before Kronos which shifts from index 6 to 7; Kronos tests in weapons.test.ts AND integration tests in rules.test.ts updated for the new index. behavior:{kind:'boomerang'}; perLevel L1–L5: damage 30→38→46→56→68 (heavy), cooldown 1.60→1.55→1.50→1.45→1.40 (commitment cadence — slower than melee but spammable enough to sustain a pool trail), constant outboundDistance:7/outboundSpeed:14/returnSpeed:18 across all levels (movement profile is the identity), hitCooldownPerEnemyMs:300 across all levels (per-axe-per-enemy gate against double-hit on outbound+return crossings — US-011 infra), leavesBloodPool flips false→false→true→true→true (THE L3 POWER SPIKE — pool trail is the identity, akin to Kronos's L4 stronger-slow spike), bloodPoolDamagePerTick stepping 0/0/4/6/8 (grows with level past L3); constant pool params bloodPoolTickIntervalMs:300, bloodPoolLifetimeMs:1500, bloodPoolSpawnIntervalUnits:1.5. No client work — BoomerangSwarm + BloodPoolSwarm from US-011 already render Bloody Axe correctly; the spinning red box and translucent red discs are the placeholder visuals. Tests (9 new): 6 in weapons.test.ts (kind index 6 + boomerang behavior, leavesBloodPool L3 power-spike pattern, bloodPoolDamagePerTick growth 4→6→8, damage strictly grows L1→L5, constant outboundDistance/Speed/returnSpeed across levels, returnSpeed > outboundSpeed sanity); 3 in rules.test.ts (L1 fires through tickWeapons emitting BoomerangThrownEvent + pushing Boomerang with leavesBloodPool:false; L3 emits leavesBloodPool:true confirming the power-spike threshold; out-of-range enemy gates fire — AD10 cooldown stays at 0). NO name-based branching (rule 12 grep clean — 'Bloody Axe' as data only). pnpm typecheck + 246 shared (was 237) / 34 client / 43 server tests pass.

userStories.title: Damage number color coding + perLevel scaling tuning pass
userStories.description: As a player, I want damage numbers to immediately communicate which weapon hit and what kind of hit (crit, slow tick, pierce). And all 6 new weapons' per-level scaling values should land at sensible starting points for playtest.
acceptanceCriteria: damage_dealt event (or a derived field on the client) carries enough info to color the number — implemented as a generic 'tag' enum field on damage_dealt: 'crit' | 'status' | 'pierce' | 'default'
Client damage-number rendering dispatches color/size from the tag — NO name-based branching (rule 12); e.g. weaponName === 'Kronos' anywhere in the renderer is forbidden
Crit hits (Damascus): YELLOW, ~1.4× font size
Status-applying hits (Kronos slow tick): ICY BLUE, 1.0× size
Pierce hits (Ahlspiess): WHITE with subtle additive glow, 1.0× size
Default: WHITE, 1.0× size
Server emits the correct tag per-hit: melee_arc crit hits → 'crit'; aura slow ticks → 'status'; pierce projectile hits (Ahlspiess pierceCount=-1) → 'pierce'; everything else → 'default'
All 6 new weapons have perLevel arrays filled in for L1–L5 with the values implied by tasks/prd-m8-weapon-variety.md §3 (or refined values from US-001 review). Documented inline in weapons.ts.
Level-up choice pool now contains 8 weapons (Bolt, Orbit + 6 new), equally weighted by default
Manual: across many runs, each of the 8 weapons is offered at least once in choice pools (sanity check on equal weighting)
Manual: pick a weapon, level it to L5, verify per-level scaling visibly takes effect (more pierce / wider arcs / larger radius / stronger slow)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: 🛑 BLOCKING — Final playtest checkpoint
userStories.description: As the project owner (Luke), I want to play a real session with friends before declaring the milestone done — the bar is 'all weapons work and feel mechanically distinct,' NOT tuned balance. Concrete bugs are fixed; balance feedback is captured for a follow-up tuning pass.
acceptanceCriteria: Real multi-client session played end-to-end
All 8 weapons confirmed to: (a) appear in choice pools, (b) deal damage when picked, (c) have a visibly distinct visual identity
No 'obviously broken' outcomes: no weapon does zero damage; no weapon one-shots everything; no weapon crashes the client or server
Performance verified: 200 enemies + 2 players each running 4–5 weapons simultaneously holds 60fps client and 20Hz server
Two-client determinism verified by direct observation: damage numbers, projectile/boomerang/aura visualizations match between two clients
Balance feedback captured (notes file or commit message) for a separate tuning milestone — NOT addressed in this milestone
